title: Review Heartbeat Statistics On The VISU_DATA Screen
summary: Use the Heartbeat section on the VISU_DATA screen to review Last, Max, and Min heartbeat signal times between the tipper and WCS in milliseconds and determine whether a signal longer than 10 seconds is present, which the manual warns can cause operation issues due to mis-synchronization.
when_to_use: Use when troubleshooting communication-health or synchronization concerns between the tipper and WCS and when heartbeat timing evidence is needed from the operator station HMI VISU_DATA screen.
not_for: Not for performing a recovery action for heartbeat-related issues, because the source provides a warning threshold condition but no recovery path.
Not for cases where the Heartbeat section is unavailable or values are unreadable; escalate instead.

roles_and_responsibilities.responsibility: Open the VISU_DATA screen, review the Heartbeat statistics, determine whether any displayed heartbeat signal time is longer than 10 seconds, and record the displayed values for troubleshooting evidence if needed.

safety_notes: The manual warns that a heartbeat signal longer than 10 seconds can cause operation issues with the tipper due to mis-synchronization.
access_or_tools_needed: Access to the operator station HMI

access_required: Access to the operator station HMI
system_preconditions: The VISU_DATA screen is available on the operator station HMI.
The Heartbeat section is visible and readable.
external_references: Figure 4-22, Operator Station HMI Data Screen—Heartbeat Stats

visual_references.description: Heartbeat section on the VISU_DATA screen showing Last, Max, Min, and RESET.
General VISU_DATA screen layout for locating the data screen context.

steps.title: Open VISU_DATA and locate the Heartbeat section
steps.purpose: Access the HMI area that displays heartbeat statistics between the tipper and WCS.
steps.instruction: Open the VISU_DATA screen on the operator station HMI and locate the Heartbeat section that shows Last, Max, Min, and RESET.
steps.expected_result: The Heartbeat section is visible on the VISU_DATA screen.
steps.healthy_condition: The Heartbeat section is present and the Last, Max, and Min fields are readable.
steps.failure_condition: The Heartbeat section is unavailable, not visible, or the values are unreadable.

actions.label: Open VISU_DATA
actions.exact_action: Open the VISU_DATA screen on the operator station HMI.

actions.label: Locate Heartbeat section
actions.exact_action: Locate the Heartbeat section showing Last, Max, Min, and RESET.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Heartbeat section with Last, Max, Min, and RESET.
Overall VISU_DATA screen layout to orient to the data screen.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Figure 4-22 appears on page 52 in section optisweep_operation_and_maintenance_manual_final_1_4_1_2_2_visu_data_screen.

steps.title: Read the Last heartbeat value
steps.purpose: Identify the most recent heartbeat signal time.
steps.instruction: Read the Last heartbeat value and interpret it as the most recent signal time in milliseconds.
steps.expected_result: The most recent heartbeat signal time is identified from the Last field.
steps.healthy_condition: A Last value is displayed and can be read in milliseconds.
steps.failure_condition: The Last value is missing, unreadable, or cannot be interpreted from the screen.

actions.label: Read Last value
actions.exact_action: Read the Last heartbeat value and interpret it as the most recent signal time in milliseconds.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: The Last field in the Heartbeat section.

stop_or_escalate_if: The Last value is unreadable or unavailable.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: The 'Heartbeat' section provides statistics for heartbeat signals. Last is the most recent signal time.

steps.title: Read the Max heartbeat value
steps.purpose: Identify the longest heartbeat signal time shown on the screen.
steps.instruction: Read the Max heartbeat value and interpret it as the longest signal time in milliseconds.
steps.expected_result: The longest heartbeat signal time is identified from the Max field.
steps.healthy_condition: A Max value is displayed and can be read in milliseconds.
steps.failure_condition: The Max value is missing, unreadable, or cannot be interpreted from the screen.

actions.label: Read Max value
actions.exact_action: Read the Max heartbeat value and interpret it as the longest signal time in milliseconds.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: The Max field in the Heartbeat section.

stop_or_escalate_if: The Max value is unreadable or unavailable.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: The 'Heartbeat' section provides statistics for heartbeat signals. Max is the longest signal time.

steps.title: Read the Min heartbeat value
steps.purpose: Identify the shortest heartbeat signal time shown on the screen.
steps.instruction: Read the Min heartbeat value and interpret it as the shortest signal time in milliseconds.
steps.expected_result: The shortest heartbeat signal time is identified from the Min field.
steps.healthy_condition: A Min value is displayed and can be read in milliseconds.
steps.failure_condition: The Min value is missing, unreadable, or cannot be interpreted from the screen.

actions.label: Read Min value
actions.exact_action: Read the Min heartbeat value and interpret it as the shortest signal time in milliseconds.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: The Min field in the Heartbeat section.

stop_or_escalate_if: The Min value is unreadable or unavailable.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: The 'Heartbeat' section provides statistics for heartbeat signals. Min is the shortest signal time.

steps.title: Check for heartbeat signal time longer than 10 seconds
steps.purpose: Determine whether the documented warning condition is present.
steps.instruction: Check whether any displayed heartbeat signal time is longer than 10 seconds, using the source warning that signals longer than 10 seconds can cause operation issues with the tipper due to mis-synchronization.
steps.expected_result: You determine whether the longer-than-10-seconds condition is present.
steps.healthy_condition: No displayed heartbeat signal time indicates a duration longer than 10 seconds.
steps.failure_condition: A displayed heartbeat signal time is longer than 10 seconds.

actions.label: Evaluate heartbeat timing
actions.exact_action: Check whether any displayed heartbeat signal time is longer than 10 seconds.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Displayed Last, Max, and Min heartbeat timing values in the Heartbeat section.

stop_or_escalate_if: A heartbeat signal longer than 10 seconds is observed because the manual states this can cause operation issues with the tipper due to mis-synchronization.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: The Heartbeat section provides Last, Max, and Min signal times in milliseconds, and a signal longer than 10 seconds can cause operation issues with the tipper due to mis-synchronization.

steps.title: Record displayed heartbeat values for troubleshooting evidence
steps.purpose: Preserve the observed heartbeat statistics for troubleshooting or escalation.
steps.instruction: Record the displayed Last, Max, and Min values if needed for troubleshooting evidence.
steps.expected_result: The displayed heartbeat values are documented for later review or escalation.
steps.healthy_condition: Last, Max, and Min values are captured accurately from the screen.
steps.failure_condition: The values cannot be captured because they are unavailable or unreadable.

actions.label: Capture displayed values
actions.exact_action: Record the displayed Last, Max, and Min values if needed for troubleshooting evidence.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: The Last, Max, and Min values to be recorded.

stop_or_escalate_if: The displayed values cannot be read or recorded.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: The 'Heartbeat' section provides statistics for heartbeat signals. Last is the most recent signal time, Max is the longest signal time, and Min is the shortest signal time.

success_criteria: The Heartbeat section on the VISU_DATA screen is located.
The Last, Max, and Min heartbeat values are read and interpreted as milliseconds.
A determination is made about whether any displayed heartbeat signal time is longer than 10 seconds.
Displayed values are recorded if needed for troubleshooting evidence.
healthy_conditions: Heartbeat statistics are visible and readable on the VISU_DATA screen.
No displayed heartbeat signal time is longer than 10 seconds.
failure_conditions: A heartbeat signal longer than 10 seconds is observed.
escalation_guidance: Escalate if a heartbeat signal longer than 10 seconds is observed because the source states this can cause operation issues with the tipper due to mis-synchronization.
Escalate if the Heartbeat section is unavailable or values are unreadable.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Heartbeat section showing Last, Max, Min, and RESET.

source_refs.quote_or_summary: The Heartbeat section provides Last, Max, and Min signal times in milliseconds, and a signal longer than 10 seconds can cause operation issues with the tipper due to mis-synchronization.
Figure 4-22 appears on page 52 in section optisweep_operation_and_maintenance_manual_final_1_4_1_2_2_visu_data_screen.

The 'Heartbeat' section provides statistics for heartbeat signals. Last is the most recent signal time, Max is the longest signal time, Min is the shortest signal time, and a signal longer than 10 seconds can cause operation issues with the tipper due to mis-synchronization.

missing_details: The source does not provide a recovery procedure or corrective action if the longer-than-10-seconds condition is present.
The source does not provide a documented normal operating threshold for Last, Max, or Min other than the warning about signals longer than 10 seconds.
The source does not provide an estimated completion time.
The source mentions RESET in the Heartbeat section artifact retrieval text, but the candidate does not include using RESET and no reset action is included here.
